title: Constants, Literals, Domain Types, and Configuration · Transfer Build
summary: Replace ambiguous magic values without creating meaningless constant noise. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a domain constant and configuration policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish harmless local literals from repeated domain rules, units, limits, and protocol values.
Name stable domain values by meaning and keep them near their owning policy.
Use enums, unions, literal types, branded values, or value objects for closed domains.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:

# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.
